rationale: EV-001 shows the deletion of a vim/editor swap file (/etc/systemd/system/.[x].service.swp), which is a standard artifact left behind when a text editor closes after editing a systemd unit file. A0 shows a subsequent 'sudo systemctl daemon-reload', which is the canonical follow-up action after editing a systemd service file to apply changes. Together these two events represent a routine, expected workflow: an administrator edited a systemd service file using a text editor (as root via pts/1), the editor cleaned up its swap file, and the admin reloaded the daemon. No evidence of malicious service content, lateral movement, persistence of a novel service, or anomalous process ancestry is present in the package.
